{{Quote|The Force guides my controls!|[[Delta-7 Aethersprite-class light interceptor/Legends|Delta-7]] pilot|audio=Forcepilot.flac|<ref name="Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds">''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]''</ref>}}
A '''Jedi ace''' was a specialized path that could be taken by any [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] pursuant to becoming a [[Jedi Guardian/Legends|Jedi Guardian]]. Each a member of the [[Jedi Starfighter Corps]], aces combined their connection to [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] with their [[Pilot/Legends|piloting]] skills to be able to perform almost impossible and daring maneuvers to down their enemies or avoid being downed themselves.

Those Jedi who pursued this manner of training would fly in specialized [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] in order to master the art of space [[Starfighter combat/Legends|combat]]. While the aces flew for the [[Army of Light/Legends|Army of Light]] and along side the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]] in the [[New Sith Wars]], the [[Ruusan Reformations/Legends|Ruusan Reformation]] saw a change in the make up of the Republic's defense forces. Now reliant on sector and planetary forces to ferry the non-[[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] capable vessels between the stars, the aces still served proudly. It wasn't until the Guardian and Ace, [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Crix Sunburris]] put the aces' role in the Order into text, with his authoring of a section of ''[[The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force]]''.<ref name="TJP" />

==Overview==
Flying on instinct and trust in the Force, victory in battle was achieved through a special Force-bond known as a [[Force-meld|Force meld]], a binding of two or more minds to coordinate attacks. [[Battle meditation/Legends|Battle meditation]] was also frequently employed by those working with the aces, so that the pilots became an extension of the coordinator's battle plan. Some Jedi aces would use the Force not only to enhance their own flying but also to influence the mind of their opponents, causing them to be confused or to lose concentration. [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] did this once while engaging in a dogfight with [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] forces, but had serious doubts afterward whether that technique was too [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark]].<ref name="Heir to the Empire">''[[Heir to the Empire]]''</ref>

Many Jedi from both the Old and [[New Jedi Order]] served proudly as an ace in the fight against the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] and doers of evil. Before he was barely a member of the Order, future ace [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] received a patch from Corps member Master [[Shaptives]] following his heroism in the [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|Battle of Naboo]] in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]. Later, [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Ahsoka Tano/Legends|Ahsoka Tano]] would detail in her edition of ''The Jedi Path'' that she had aspirations of joining this elite group, but would never do so due to her [[Trial of Ahsoka Tano/Legends|leaving]] the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]<ref name="The Wrong Jedi">{{TCW|The Wrong Jedi}}</ref> and the subsequent [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]].<ref name="TJP" /> Following the Order's rebirth, [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] Skywalker, his wife [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara]], their niece [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]], [[Kyp Durron]], and [[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn]] were all considered among the finest the Jedi academy had produced.<ref name="The New Jedi Order Sourcebook">''[[The New Jedi Order Sourcebook]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Jedi Ace is a prestige class in the ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]''.
